content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

June 26, 2009

Mark Rahlwes
County of Orange, Auditor-Controller
Finance and Administrative Services
12 Civic Center Plaza, Room 200
Santa Ana, California 92701

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-09-089

Dear Mr. Rahlwes:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest code provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ We received your request for advice dated March 31, 2009. In that request you sought advice on how to proceed with disputing the full disclosure requirement for your position with the County of Orange Auditor-Controller in the Finance & Administrative Section. The Orange County Board of Supervisors is your agency's code reviewing body. Regulation 18329.5 requires that where the Commission is not the code reviewing body for the conflict of interest code in question, as the requesting party you must first request a determination from your agency and the code reviewing body for your agency's conflict of interest code. I informed you during our telephone conversation on April 23, 2009, that we would hold your request for advice until you received a determination on this issue from your agency and the code reviewing body for your agency's conflict of interest code. I then sent confirmation of this in writing on May 4, 2009, and asked you to forward me this information once it was available. We have not received any further correspondence from you regarding this matter and are withdrawing your request for advice at this time. Please feel free to resubmit your request should you receive the required information that we previously discussed.

Sincerely,

Sukhi K. Brar
Counsel, Legal Division

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

May 4, 2009

Mark Rahlwes
County of Orange, Auditor-Controller
Finance and Administrative Services
12 Civic Center Plaza Room 200
Santa Ana, California 92701

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 09-089

Dear Mr. Rahlwes:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest code provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ We are in receipt of your request for advice dated March 31, 2009. In that request you sought advice on how to proceed with disputing the full disclosure requirement for your position with the County of Orange Auditor-Controller in the Finance & Administrative section. The Orange County Board of supervisors is your agency's code reviewing body. Regulation 18329.5 requires that where the Commission is not the code reviewing body for the conflict of interest code in question, as the requesting party, you must first request a determination from your agency and the code reviewing body for such conflict of interest code. As I informed you during our telephone conversation on April 23, 2009, at this time we will hold your request for advice until you have received a determination on this issue from your agency and the code reviewing body for your agency's conflict of interest code. Please contact me when you have received the required determinations or when the time period has expired for the required responses.

Sincerely,

Sukhi K. Brar
Counsel, Legal Division
